username.description: Username to grant the instance-admin role to.

rangeStart.description: Earliest DataObject anchor timestamp considered in this response, ISO-8601 UTC. Null when the Collection has no DataObjects.

rangeEnd.description: Latest DataObject anchor timestamp considered in this response, ISO-8601 UTC. Null when the Collection has no DataObjects.

targetKind.description: OGM label of the target entity, when known.

agentUsername.description: Acting Agent's display name (UUID-shaped Keycloak subjects are redacted to their first 8 chars).

startedAtMillis.description: Millis since epoch when the activity began.

endedAtMillis.description: Millis since epoch when the activity ended.

method.description: HTTP method that drove the activity, when captured via REST.

path.description: Request path that drove the activity, when captured via REST.

status.description: HTTP status code of the resulting response, when captured via REST.

originInstance.description: Origin-instance identifier — 'local' by default; Edge-instance UUID on synced rows.

sourceMode.description: PROV1j — agent mode: 'human' or 'ai'. Null for pre-PROV1j rows (treat as 'human').

agentId.description: PROV1j — AI agent identifier from X-AI-Agent header (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6'). Null for human callers.

jobId.description: Stable UUID v7 identifier for this anchor job.

message.description: Human-readable summary, e.g. '3/3 activities anchored via bloxberg.'.

value.description: The channel value at this timestamp.

interpolated.description: true when this point was linearly interpolated at the window boundary; false for raw data.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

mimeType.description: MIME type of the video file, e.g. video/mp4, video/quicktime.

width.description: Pixel width of the video stream. Null if unknown.

height.description: Pixel height of the video stream. Null if unknown.

frameRate.description: Frames per second. Null if unknown.

videoCodec.description: Video codec name, e.g. h264, vp9. Null if unknown.

audioCodec.description: Audio codec name, e.g. aac, opus. Null if unknown.

mode.description: Use-mode (aidocs/38 §2). LOOSE_LINK (default) renders as a clickable URL; TRACKED_ARTIFACT enables the server-side inline preview via GET /v2/data-objects/{do}/git-references/{appId}/preview; PINNED_SNAPSHOT is reserved for G1c.

ref.description: Branch name, tag, or HEAD. Null = repository default.

path.description: Path inside the repository. Null = repository root.

sha.description: Resolved commit SHA for mode-(c) pinned snapshots. Always null in mode (a) v1; populated by G1c.

resolvedSha.description: SHA that fulfilled `ref` at the last fetch (mode (b)). Always null in v1; populated by G1b.

resolvedAtMillis.description: Epoch-millis when `resolvedSha` was captured. Null in mode (a) v1.

AnomalyDetectRequestIO.description: Request parameters for the rolling-median MAD anomaly detector.

window.description: Rolling window size (data points). Must be ≥ 3. Forced odd. Clamped to series length if too large. Default: 51.

k.description: Anomaly threshold on the Z-score (|z| > k is flagged). Must be > 0. Default: 6.0.

createAnnotations.description: If true, persist a TimeseriesAnnotation per detected interval (label 'anomaly', aiGenerated=true). Requires Write permission. Default: false.

measurement.description: Measurement tag — selects which series in the reference to score. Omit when the reference contains exactly one series.

device.description: Device tag — selects which series in the reference to score.

location.description: Location tag — selects which series in the reference to score.

symbolicName.description: SymbolicName tag — selects which series in the reference to score.

field.description: Field tag — selects which series in the reference to score.

detectorId.description: Detector identifier (AT1). Default 'mad-v1' (the rolling-median MAD detector). Future detectors register via shepard-plugin-analytics-ts and become reachable through this field.

pointCount.description: Total data points across all channels in this container.

recentPointsLast10s.description: Data points inserted in the last 10 seconds.

ingestRateBytesPerSec.description: Estimated ingest rate in bytes per second (recentPointsLast10s × 28 / 10).

activeProviderId.description: Id of the currently active storage adapter, or 'none' when no adapter is configured.

adapters.description: All discovered storage adapters with their id, enabled state, and active flag.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

created.description: Templates created or updated during this import run.

skipped.description: Count of templates skipped because their content was already current.

key.description: Stable slug for the lane (e.g. "afp-layup", "ndt-inspection", "unclassified"). Derived from the annotation valueName.

bins.description: Day-bins ordered ascending by day; empty when the lane has no DataObjects.

snapshotACapturedAtMs.description: Epoch milliseconds at which snapshot A was captured.

snapshotBCapturedAtMs.description: Epoch milliseconds at which snapshot B was captured.

added.description: entityAppIds present in B but not in A (created between snapshots). Sorted ascending.

removed.description: entityAppIds present in A but not in B (deleted or out of scope in B). Sorted ascending.

unchangedCount.description: Count of entities present in both snapshots with identical revisions (not listed to avoid large payloads).

host.description: Git host, no scheme. Example: gitlab.com

username.description: Git username this credential is valid for.

in.description: sh:in membership list. Null/empty = no sh:in.

pattern.description: Regex the literal value must match (sh:pattern).

hints.description: Non-validating form-presentation hints (DASH editor, label, order, group, placeholder, cell mapping — doc 125 §4).

sharedAnnotations.description: List of annotation key-value pairs appearing on at least one DataObject in each set (empty when independent). Shared annotations may indicate the sets were derived from the same experimental context.

checkedAt.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the check was performed.

enabled.description: Whether this adapter is currently usable (credentials present, upstream reachable).

active.description: Whether this adapter is the currently active provider for new uploads.

keys.description: Ordered key sequence identifying the referenced element.

displayName.description: Human-readable DataObject name for display. Null when not populated.

dataObjectAppIds.description: DataObject appIds (UUID v7 strings) to resolve channels under. Max 100.

downsampleTo.description: LTTB target rows per series. Minimum 1, default 500 (when null or omitted), hard cap 5000.

doCount.description: DataObject count on this child Collection.

lastActivityMillis.description: Maximum {created/updated} timestamp on this child Collection; epoch millis.

alsoMemberOf.description: Other Projects this Collection is also a member of (urn:shepard:partOf appIds, excluding the queried parent).

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

downloadUrl.description: Presigned GET URL. Issue an HTTP GET to this URL to download the file bytes directly from storage. No authentication headers are required.

entityAppId.description: appId of the entity that changed revision between snapshots.

revisionA.description: Revision the entity held in snapshot A (base).

revisionB.description: Revision the entity held in snapshot B (head).

attributeKey.description: DataObject attribute key when the path lives in the urn:shepard:attribute: namespace — the key a client puts in the instantiation request's attributes map. Null for non-attribute paths.

label.description: Field label (sh:name; falls back to the path's local name).

group.description: IRI of the group this field belongs to (sh:group).

editor.description: Editor IRI — explicit dash:editor, else the DASH constraint-scoring default.

cellMapping.description: Excel cell mapping (urn:btkvs:cell-mapping + urn:btkvs:sheet). Domain-side, BTKVS-C2.

pluginId.description: Sibling plugin id this plugin depends on.

fileReferenceAppId.description: Singleton FileReference appId of the .OTvis archive.

height.description: Image height in pixels (first decoded frame).

partialReason.description: Fail-soft tolerance notes from the extractor; null when extraction was lossless.

role.description: Renderer-facing role name (x, y, z, time, color, size, …). Conventions are per-renderer-family; Trace3D uses x/y/z/color.

channelSelector.description: Channel selector as declared in the template. Today: JSON-encoded 5-tuple {measurement, device, location, symbolicName, field}. Post TS-ID migration (aidocs/platform/87): a single appId string.

unit.description: Expected QUDT unit IRI (e.g. 'http://qudt.org/vocab/unit/MilliM'). Null when the template declares no unit expectation.

required.description: If true the renderer cannot operate when this binding is MISSING. If false the renderer degrades gracefully (omits the dimension).

status.description: Resolution status: DECLARED (beta) | OK | MISSING | UNIT_MISMATCH. In this beta release always DECLARED — live resolution ships in TPL2c.

resolved.description: Resolved channel reference. Null in the beta release. In TPL2c: non-null when status=OK, containing the TS reference appId.

channelRef.description: appId of the TimeseriesReference node whose channel list satisfies this binding.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

untilMillis.description: Inclusive upper bound of the window (millis since epoch).

distinctAgents.description: Distinct contributor count over the window (instance-wide or scope-narrowed).

contentCensus.description: Entity-count census for the scope at query time (NOT window-filtered). Keys: dataObjects, fileReferences, timeseriesReferences, structuredDataReferences, spatialDataReferences, labJournalEntries. Drives the casual-user dashboard's 'what's in here' tiles. Null when scope=user (the census is not per-user).

byteTotals.description: Byte-size totals for the scope at query time (NOT window-filtered). Keys: `fileBytes` (sum of `ShepardFile.fileSize` reachable from the scope). Pre-FB1a rows uploaded before `fileSize` capture landed have `fileSize=null` and contribute 0 to the sum — the total is therefore a **lower bound** until those rows are re-uploaded. Null when scope=user (the byte totals are not per-user). Open-shape: future kinds (`timeseriesBytes`, `structuredDataBytes`) get added as separate keys without changing the schema.

name.description: Human-readable name for the new DataObject. Defaults to template name + timestamp suffix when omitted.

kind.description: IRI or LITERAL. Null defaults to LITERAL.

datatype.description: Literal datatype IRI (LITERAL only). Null defaults to xsd:string.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

ProcessChainMappingResultIO.description: Result of applying a MFFD process-chain mapping YAML payload.

entries.description: Total mapping entries in the YAML payload.

matched.description: Sum of (source × target) DataObject pairs matched across all entries.

unmatched.description: Number of entries where either side resolved zero DataObjects.

edgesCreated.description: Number of has_successor edges MERGEd (created or refreshed) by this run.

unresolved.description: Per-entry checklist of unresolved selectors, with YAML line numbers.

templateKind.description: Template kind, e.g. DATAOBJECT_RECIPE / COLLECTION_RECIPE / EXPERIMENT_RECIPE.

version.description: Copy-on-write version number; latest non-retired row has the highest value for the name.

body.description: Recipe body. JSON DSL per aidocs/54 §7 (opaque to the entity; validation is service-layer). Optional field `shapeGraph` (Turtle string) is read by /shapes/validate per SHAPES-V-PREFILL-3-EXTRACT-SHACL.

createdBy.description: Display name of the user who minted (or copy-on-write-edited) the row (UUID-shaped Keycloak subjects are redacted).

createdAt.description: Millis since epoch when the row was created.

updatedAt.description: Millis since epoch when the row was last touched.

retired.description: true when retired and filtered from picker listings (still kept on disk).

iconKey.description: MDI (Material Design Icons) name with the 'mdi-' prefix, e.g. 'mdi-layers'. Null means the UI uses the per-kind default. Design: aidocs/integrations/122.

parentTemplateAppId.description: appId of the parent template this template extends (single-parent inheritance). Null means a root template. Child fields override parent on collision. Design: aidocs/integrations/123.

HintsIO.description: Non-validating form hints on one property shape (doc 125 §4).

group.description: IRI of the sh:PropertyGroup this field belongs to (sh:group).

OntologyGitSourceIO.description: An ontology Git source — a repository that Shepard polls for ontology files.

appId.description: Stable application-level identifier (UUID v7). Read-only; set on create.

repoUrl.description: Full git clone URL. Accepted: https:// or git@host:path.

branch.description: Branch to clone. Defaults to 'main' when not supplied.

pathPattern.description: Glob pattern for ontology files within the cloned repo. A bare pattern like '*.ttl' is matched at any depth; a path-qualified one like 'ontologies/*.owl' is relative to the repo root.

targetRepoAppId.description: appId of the SemanticRepository to ingest into. Currently informational — all uploads target the internal n10s store.

enabled.description: When false the nightly scheduler skips this source. Default true.

lastIngestedAt.description: Epoch-ms of the last completed ingest run. Null if never run.

lastStatus.description: Last ingest status: OK, ERROR, or PENDING. Null if never run.

lastError.description: Last error message. Null when lastStatus is OK.

createdAt.description: Epoch-ms when this record was created.

createdBy.description: Username of the admin who created this record.

total.description: Total matched entities across all kinds.

page.description: Zero-based page index (applies to collection results).

pageSize.description: Page size used for this request.

AnnotationV2.description: SEMA-V6-004 — full v6 semantic annotation response shape.

subjectKind.description: Kind of the annotated entity (e.g. 'DataObject', 'Collection', 'FileReference'). Null on legacy rows.

subjectAppId.description: appId of the annotated entity. Null on legacy rows.

predicateLabel.description: Human-readable label snapshot of the predicate.

vocabularyId.description: appId of the Vocabulary that defines this predicate. Null means free-form.

objectLiteral.description: Literal text value of the annotation. Exactly one of objectLiteral / objectIri is non-null.

objectIri.description: IRI-typed object value. Exactly one of objectLiteral / objectIri is non-null.

sourceMode.description: f(ai)²r provenance mode: 'human', 'ai', or 'collaborative'. Null on legacy rows.

sourceActivityAppId.description: appId of the Activity that captured this annotation write.

validFromMillis.description: Epoch-millis at which this annotation becomes valid. Null = valid from creation.

validUntilMillis.description: Epoch-millis at which this annotation expires. Null = no expiry (currently asserted).

confidence.description: Confidence score [0.0, 1.0]. Null = not specified (human writes default 1.0).

propertyName.description: Legacy: human-readable predicate label snapshot (mapped from propertyName).

propertyIri.description: Legacy: predicate IRI (mapped from propertyIRI; same as predicateIri).

valueName.description: Legacy: object label snapshot (mapped from valueName).

valueIri.description: Legacy: object IRI (mapped from valueIRI; same as objectIri).

validUntil.description: Optional expiry of this api key. If null, the key never expires.

roles.description: Roles to mint this API key with. Each role must be held by the caller and appear in shepard.apikey.role-allowlist. Empty / null = no role claims (default).

name.description: If set, replaces the human-readable name.

body.description: If set, replaces the JSON DSL body.

tags.description: If set, replaces the tag list (full replace, not merge).

iconKey.description: If set, replaces the MDI icon name (e.g. 'mdi-layers'). The empty string clears it (UI falls back to per-kind default). Design: aidocs/integrations/122.

parentTemplateAppId.description: If set, replaces the parent template appId (single-parent inheritance). The empty string clears it (template becomes a root). Must share this template's templateKind; cycles are rejected. Design: aidocs/integrations/123.

DataObjectListItemV2.description: DataObject list item enriched with per-kind reference counts (v2).

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

description.example: Post-hotfire analysis of turbopump vibration anomaly at t=8s (TR-004)

createdByOrcid.description: FAIR2: ORCID of the creator, stamped server-side at creation. Read-only — not accepted as input. Absent when null or creator had no ORCID set.

embargoEndDate.description: ISO-8601 date (e.g. '2027-12-31') after which the embargo lifts; only meaningful when accessRights=EMBARGOED.

predecessorAppIds.description: BUG-PREDECESSOR-IDS-NUMERIC-IN-V2-PATCH: appId (UUID v7) array of predecessor DataObjects. On PATCH bodies, when non-null and non-empty, overrides predecessorIds. On GET responses, populated alongside predecessorIds; nulls filtered for pre-L2b rows.

timeseriesReferenceCount.description: Deprecated — use `timeseriesCount` on the /v2/ DataObjectListItemV2 shape instead. This int includes soft-deleted references; the v2 long field counts non-deleted only.

fileBundleCount.description: Deprecated — use `fileCount` on the /v2/ DataObjectListItemV2 shape instead. This int counts only FileBundleReference nodes (FR1a); the v2 long also counts SingletonFileReference (FR1b) and excludes soft-deleted references.

structuredDataReferenceCount.description: Deprecated — use `structuredDataCount` on the /v2/ DataObjectListItemV2 shape instead. This int includes soft-deleted references; the v2 long field counts non-deleted only.

attachedTemplateAppId.description: TOOLS-CONTEXT-DO-TEMPLATE-DETECT-1: appId of the :ShepardTemplate this DataObject was created from. Stamped server-side at create time; read-only. Absent from the wire when null.

timeseriesCount.description: Number of non-deleted TimeseriesReferences attached to this DataObject.

fileCount.description: Number of non-deleted file references (FileReference + SingletonFileReference) attached to this DataObject.

structuredDataCount.description: Number of non-deleted StructuredDataReferences attached to this DataObject.

timeBoundsStart.description: Earliest data-point timestamp across all timeseries channels of this DataObject, in nanoseconds since Unix epoch. Null when no timeseries data exists or `?include=time-bounds` was not requested.

timeBoundsEnd.description: Latest data-point timestamp across all timeseries channels of this DataObject, in nanoseconds since Unix epoch. Null when no timeseries data exists or `?include=time-bounds` was not requested.

provenanceMode.description: EU AI Act Art. 50 provenance mode: 'human', 'ai', 'collaborative', or null (default, equivalent to human-authored). Set from the X-AI-Agent request header on create.

parentAppId.description: appId (UUID v7) of the parent DataObject, or null when top-level. appId-native linkage for the sidebar tree (replaces the suppressed numeric parentId).

childrenAppIds.description: appIds (UUID v7) of direct non-deleted children (empty for a leaf). appId-native linkage for the sidebar tree (replaces the suppressed numeric childrenIds).

TemplateFormDescriptor.description: Compiled form descriptor for a data-kind ShepardTemplate (doc 125 §5.1).

templateAppId.description: appId of the template this descriptor was compiled from.

templateKind.description: The template's kind (a data kind: DATAOBJECT_RECIPE / COLLECTION_RECIPE / STRUCTURED_RECIPE).

fields.description: Form fields, ordered by sh:order (unordered fields last, alphabetical — the DASH rule).

submit.description: Server-computed submit target. The client never chooses an endpoint.

containerRef.description: localRef of the Container to attach.

watchedCollectionCount.description: Number of collections the caller is currently watching (CW1).

anonymizeInProvenance.description: PROV1l — when true, identity is omitted from :Activity records (GDPR opt-out). Default false.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

ContainerRef.description: Compact link from a DataObject reference node to its container.

referenceAppId.description: UUID v7 of the reference node. Null on pre-L2a rows.

TypedPredecessor.description: PROV1k — typed predecessor relationship. predecessorAppId: UUID v7 appId of the predecessor DataObject (same collection). relationshipType: PROV-O / FAIR²R predicate — 'prov:wasInformedBy' (default), 'prov:wasRevisionOf', 'fair2r:repairs', or 'fair2r:concession' (QM1b concession / use-as-is disposition).

predecessorAppId.description: UUID v7 appId of the predecessor DataObject (must be in the same collection).

relationshipType.description: PROV-O or FAIR²R relationship type. Null or absent defaults to 'prov:wasInformedBy'. Allowed values: 'prov:wasInformedBy', 'prov:wasRevisionOf', 'fair2r:repairs', 'fair2r:concession'.

hdfContainerAppId.description: appId of the target HdfContainer (UUID v7).

datasetPath.description: HDF5 dataset path within the container (e.g. "/sensor_data/channel_A").

AnomalyDetectResultIO.description: Result of a rolling-median MAD anomaly detection run.

anomalies.description: List of detected contiguous anomaly intervals. Empty when the series is clean.

windowSize.description: Effective rolling window size used (after odd-forcing and series-length clamping).

threshold.description: Anomaly Z-score threshold used (k). A point is anomalous when |z| > threshold.

totalPoints.description: Total number of data points in the series that were evaluated.

annotationsCreated.description: Number of TimeseriesAnnotation nodes created (0 when createAnnotations=false).

container_id_in.description: Deprecated — use container_app_id_in (UUID v7 strings) instead. Numeric Neo4j container IDs; silently ignored when container_app_id_in is also present.

container_app_id_in.description: Timeseries container appIds (UUID v7) to include. Unknown or unauthorised appIds are silently excluded.

ProjectByAnnotationIO.description: Cross-Collection by-annotation roll-up for a Project.

predicate.description: Predicate IRI used for the filter.

totalCount.description: Total number of matching DataObjects across the Project.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

name.description: Stable identifier for the feature toggle.

enabled.description: Whether the feature is currently enabled.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the toggle controls.

source.description: Origin of the current value: 'runtime' (PATCH-overridden, reverts on restart), 'config' (from application.properties / env), or 'default' (hardcoded default, no explicit config key).

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

title.description: Human-readable display name; falls back to id when blank.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

CollectionHeroViewLinkIO.description: Collection hero-view link: the MAPPING_RECIPE template appId that renders as the Collection's hero 3D view.

sceneGraphAppId.description: appId of the linked MAPPING_RECIPE template (the hero view).

templateName.description: Name of the linked template (decorative; null when the template was deleted).

templateKind.description: templateKind of the linked template — always MAPPING_RECIPE for a valid hero view.

UploadItemSchema.description: File which you want to upload

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

frameAppId.description: Optional CoordinateFrame appId (CST1) this container is anchored in.

IndependenceProofRequestIO.description: Two sets of DataObject appIds to check for independence.

setA.description: AppIds of DataObjects in set A (e.g. training set). Must contain at least 1 element.

setB.description: AppIds of DataObjects in set B (e.g. test set). Must contain at least 1 element.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

available.description: True if the preview was fetched successfully.

reason.description: When available=false, a short machine-readable reason: no-credential / unsupported-host / not-tracked / fetch-failed.

sha.description: Commit SHA that fulfilled `ref` at the fetch.

mimeType.description: MIME type as reported by the git host (charset suffix stripped).

byteSize.description: Total file size in bytes as reported by the git host.

content.description: UTF-8 file content. Null when contentTruncated=true or available=false.

contentTruncated.description: True if the file exceeds shepard.git.preview.max-bytes (default 1 MB); content is null and the client should fall back to an `open in <host>` link.

containerAppId.description: appId of the HdfContainer that failed to sync.

DataObjectBatchResultItem.description: Per-item outcome within a batch create response (HTTP 207 Multi-Status). On success: status='created', appId populated. On failure: status='error', errorCode + errorMessage populated.

index.description: Zero-based position of this item in the input array.

status.description: Outcome: "created" on success, "error" on per-item failure.

appId.description: The appId (UUID v7) minted for the created DataObject. Null (omitted) when status is "error".

errorCode.description: Machine-readable error code. Null (omitted) when status is "created". Known values: COLLECTION_NOT_FOUND, FORBIDDEN, INVALID_INPUT, PARENT_NOT_FOUND, INTERNAL_ERROR.

errorMessage.description: Human-readable error description. Null (omitted) when status is "created".

appId.description: Application-level identifier of the Publication row (UUID v7).

pid.description: The minted persistent identifier — string the active minter returned.

mintedAt.description: Server-side wall-clock at the moment the minter returned.

minterId.description: Identifier of the minter that produced this row (e.g. 'local', 'epic').

resolverUrl.description: The fully-qualified URL where this PID can be dereferenced at this shepard instance — points at the public /v2/.well-known/kip resolver.

entityKind.description: URL-segment of the published entity's kind (e.g. 'data-objects').

versionNumber.description: KIP1h Phase-1 version number. 1-based ordinal of this Publication among the entity's :Publication rows.

digitalObjectMutability.description: KIP1f mutability marker. null on active Publications; 'retired' after DELETE /v2/{kind}/{appId}/publish. Read-only — the client cannot set this field; use the DELETE endpoint to retire.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

file.description: The single attached file. May be null in degenerate-row cases (missing GridFS blob).

fileKind.description: V2CONV-A2 file-kind discriminator derived from the original filename extension + mime (e.g. "krl", "svdx", "otvis", "urdf", "xit", "pdf"). Null when the kind is unrecognised or the singleton predates this field.

updated.description: Number of SemanticAnnotation nodes whose propertyName or valueName snapshot was updated.

ShapeBuildRequestIO.description: JSON DSL for a SHACL NodeShape. Compiled to canonical Turtle by POST /v2/shapes/build.

shapeIri.description: IRI of the sh:NodeShape. When null/blank the builder mints a deterministic local IRI.

targetClass.description: sh:targetClass IRI. Nullable — a shape may target by other means or be referenced via sh:node.

closed.description: sh:closed — when true, emits sh:closed true.

properties.description: The property constraints. Null/empty = a shape with no property constraints.

groups.description: sh:PropertyGroup declarations (form sections, doc 125 §4.2). Null/empty = none.

targetNode.description: sh:targetNode IRI. Shapes meant to fire on the validate-on-instantiate seam target urn:shepard:instance:candidate (the stable candidate-graph URI).

requested.description: Number of bundles considered (matches the request's filter, or all bundles when no filter was given).

refreshed.description: Number of bundles whose canonical Turtle was successfully fetched and re-imported into n10s.

alreadyCurrent.description: Number of bundles skipped because the canonical Turtle hash already matched the bundled stub (force=false).

errors.description: Per-bundle errors; empty when every refresh succeeded.

startNs.description: Start of the annotated interval in nanoseconds since Unix epoch.

endNs.description: End of the annotated interval in nanoseconds since Unix epoch. Null for point annotations.

aiGenerated.description: True when created by the anomaly-detection pipeline, false for user-created annotations.

confidence.description: Confidence score [0.0, 1.0] from anomaly detection. Null for human-created annotations.

appId.description: Application-level UUID v7 identifier of the snapshot.

snapshotCapturedAt.description: ISO-8601 instant at which the snapshot was captured.

snapshotCreatedByUsername.description: Username of the caller who created the snapshot.

collectionAppId.description: appId of the root Collection that was walked when the snapshot was created.

entryCount.description: Number of SnapshotEntry rows captured (equals the number of distinct VersionableEntity nodes in scope at snapshot time).

TimeseriesChannelV2.description: Per-channel listing entry on the /v2/ surface, addressed by shepardId.

shepardId.description: Channel shepardId — the new single-field identity for this channel.

id.description: DEPRECATED: legacy numeric channel id (Postgres serial). Use shepardId instead.

containerId.description: DEPRECATED (APISIMP-TSCHANNEL-CONTAINER-ID): numeric Postgres serial FK exposed on wire. Use containerAppId once available — requires TS-IDb/c migration.

PredicateStats.description: Per-predicate usage statistics across all :SemanticAnnotation rows.

predicate.description: The predicate IRI these stats describe (echoes the request).

topValues.description: Most-used object values for this predicate, ordered by count DESC.

sampleEntities.description: Sample of entities annotated with this predicate (capped by sampleLimit).

ShapesApplicableResponseIO.description: Applicable views (mode=VIEW) and forms (mode=FORM) for one focus entity.

focusAppId.description: Echoes the focusAppId from the request.

items.description: Applicable shapes; empty when nothing applies (valid, not an error).

validUntil.description: Optional expiry of this api key. If null, the key never expires.

roles.description: Roles to mint this API key with. Each role must be held by the caller and appear in shepard.apikey.role-allowlist. Empty / null = no role claims (default).

hypertableSizeBytes.description: Total on-disk bytes for the timeseries_data_points hypertable (all chunks, compressed + uncompressed).

channelCount.description: Number of distinct timeseries channels registered across all containers.

containerCount.description: Number of distinct containers that have at least one channel.

uncompressedChunkBytes.description: Approximate uncompressed size bytes (channelCount × avg-bytes-per-point estimate is not used here; this is the sum of before_compression_total_bytes across all compressed chunks).

compressionRatio.description: Compression ratio estimate (uncompressedChunkBytes / hypertableSizeBytes). Null when no chunks have been compressed yet.

DataObjectSummary.description: Compact DataObject reference — appId, name, status, createdAt, createdBy.

createdAt.description: PRED-V2-SHAPE: creation timestamp. Null for pre-OGM rows.

createdBy.description: PRED-V2-SHAPE: display name of the creator.

collectionAppId.description: appId of the Collection this snippet was generated from.

snippet.description: Pre-filled Markdown DMP text block. Copy-paste into the data management plan form.

missingFields.description: FAIR fields that are null/blank and should be set to improve the DMP. Possible values: 'license', 'accessRights', 'orcid', 'description'. Empty list means all key fields are populated.

UpdateAnnotationV2.description: SEMA-V6-004 — merge-patch body for updating a semantic annotation.

objectLiteral.description: New literal text value (leave null to leave unchanged). If provided, objectIri is cleared.

objectIri.description: New IRI-typed object value (leave null to leave unchanged). If provided, objectLiteral is cleared.

validUntilMillis.description: Epoch-millis close of the prior validity window. Setting this to a past time soft-deletes the annotation.

sourceMode.description: Updated provenance mode for this write.

oid.description: The oid returned by the upload-url endpoint.

fileName.description: File name to register for this file.

contentType.description: MIME type of the uploaded file.

fileSize.description: File size in bytes. Optional but recommended.

status.description: Current lifecycle state of the migration job.

sourceProviderId.description: Id of the adapter being drained.

targetProviderId.description: Id of the adapter receiving files.

filesTotal.description: Total number of files to migrate (counted at job start).

filesMigrated.description: Files successfully moved to the target adapter so far.

startedAt.description: When the migration job was triggered.

errorMessage.description: Fatal error message when status is FAILED.

templateAppId.description: appId of the MAPPING_RECIPE template that was materialized.

outputKind.description: REFERENCE when a derived reference appId is returned; VIEW for a played/rendered projection.

derivedReferenceAppId.description: appId of the derived reference (set when outputKind=REFERENCE).

executor.description: Name of the TransformExecutor that produced this result.

uploadUrl.description: Presigned PUT URL. Send a single HTTP PUT request to this URL with the file bytes as the body. No authentication headers are required; the signature is embedded in the URL.

oid.description: Object identifier (UUID) assigned to this file. Pass this to the commit endpoint after the upload completes.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

AasKey.description: One key in an IDTA AAS v3 Reference key sequence.

value.description: Identifier value — the global IRI or appId of the referenced element.

content.description: Content of the entry as it existed before the edit.

revisedBy.description: Display name of the user who made the edit.

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 instant at which the snapshot was captured.

collectionName.description: Name of the root Collection (snapshotted at list time, may drift from the live entity).

DataObjectDetail.description: Full DataObject detail response, extends DataObjectIO with typed container lists and compact predecessor/successor/parent/child summaries.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

description.example: Post-hotfire analysis of turbopump vibration anomaly at t=8s (TR-004)

createdByOrcid.description: FAIR2: ORCID of the creator, stamped server-side at creation. Read-only — not accepted as input. Absent when null or creator had no ORCID set.

embargoEndDate.description: ISO-8601 date (e.g. '2027-12-31') after which the embargo lifts; only meaningful when accessRights=EMBARGOED.

predecessorAppIds.description: BUG-PREDECESSOR-IDS-NUMERIC-IN-V2-PATCH: appId (UUID v7) array of predecessor DataObjects. On PATCH bodies, when non-null and non-empty, overrides predecessorIds. On GET responses, populated alongside predecessorIds; nulls filtered for pre-L2b rows.

timeseriesReferenceCount.description: Deprecated — use `timeseriesCount` on the /v2/ DataObjectListItemV2 shape instead. This int includes soft-deleted references; the v2 long field counts non-deleted only.

fileBundleCount.description: Deprecated — use `fileCount` on the /v2/ DataObjectListItemV2 shape instead. This int counts only FileBundleReference nodes (FR1a); the v2 long also counts SingletonFileReference (FR1b) and excludes soft-deleted references.

structuredDataReferenceCount.description: Deprecated — use `structuredDataCount` on the /v2/ DataObjectListItemV2 shape instead. This int includes soft-deleted references; the v2 long field counts non-deleted only.

attachedTemplateAppId.description: TOOLS-CONTEXT-DO-TEMPLATE-DETECT-1: appId of the :ShepardTemplate this DataObject was created from. Stamped server-side at create time; read-only. Absent from the wire when null.

typedPredecessorSummaries.description: PROV1k — typed predecessor list. Null when no predecessors. Each entry carries the predecessor's summary (appId, id, name, status) and the PROV-O / FAIR²R relationship type. Pre-PROV1k predecessors default to 'prov:wasInformedBy'.

parentSummary.description: Parent DataObject summary, or null if top-level.

provenanceMode.description: EU AI Act Art. 50 provenance mode for this DataObject: 'human', 'ai', 'collaborative', or null (default, semantically equivalent to human-authored). Set automatically from the X-AI-Agent request header on create when not provided explicitly.

timeseriesReferenceAppIds.description: appIds (UUID v7) of TimeseriesReference nodes on this DataObject. Use with GET /v2/timeseries-containers/{appId} to reach the container. Null when none exist.

fileReferenceAppIds.description: appIds (UUID v7) of FileBundleReference nodes on this DataObject. Null when none exist.

structuredDataReferenceAppIds.description: appIds (UUID v7) of StructuredDataReference nodes on this DataObject. Null when none exist.

downloadUrl.description: Presigned GET URL. Issue an HTTP GET to download the RO-Crate ZIP directly from storage. No authentication headers are required. Expires after 30 minutes.

fileName.description: Suggested filename for the downloaded ZIP.

PredecessorEdgePatch.description: QM1b — set the PROV-O / FAIR²R relationship type for an existing predecessor edge. Allowed values: 'prov:wasInformedBy' (default / 'normal'), 'prov:wasRevisionOf' ('re-test'), 'fair2r:repairs' ('rework'), 'fair2r:concession' ('concession / use-as-is').

relationshipType.description: PROV-O / FAIR²R relationship type. One of: 'prov:wasInformedBy', 'prov:wasRevisionOf', 'fair2r:repairs', 'fair2r:concession'.

templateVersion.description: Template version at instantiation time (copy-on-write history is preserved).

body.description: Recipe body. JSON DSL per aidocs/54 §7 — client interprets.

edgeCreated.description: Whether the :USES_TEMPLATE edge was created on this call. False on idempotent re-stamp (the edge already existed).

ExportSelectionMetadata.description: Per-kind opt-in for bundling metadata documents. Defaults: labJournal=true (legacy); permissions/annotations/versions/subscriptions=false. Subscriptions: the exported document lists subscriptions whose URL pattern matches the entity's canonical relative URL. redactFields is a closed set of fields on the emitted IO documents that are replaced with a sentinel before they are added to the crate (privacy: post-permission-check redaction). Redaction has no effect on metadata kinds that are not also opted-in (no document emitted ⇒ nothing to redact).

redactFields.description: Closed set of fields on the emitted metadata IO documents that are replaced with a sentinel before bundling. Privacy semantics: post-permission-check redaction; the redacted field set is recorded in the manifest, the replacement values are not.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this config controls.

assetKind.description: Asset kind. Always `Instance` for Collection-backed Shells in AAS1a.

globalAssetId.description: Globally unique IRI for the underlying asset. Derived from the Collection appId: `urn:shepard:asset:{appId}`.

displayName.description: Updated human-readable label. Null = leave unchanged.

username.description: Updated git username. Null = leave unchanged.

pat.description: Updated PAT. Write-only — never returned. Null = leave unchanged.

webdavVisible.description: Whether this Collection appears in the /v2/webdav mount surface.

uiDefaultsJson.description: Free-form UI defaults JSON (opaque to the backend).

publishToHelmholtzKG.description: When false, this Collection is excluded from the Helmholtz Unhide feed (/v2/unhide/feed.jsonld). Default true (opt-out rather than opt-in). UH1d.

CopyIngestRequest.description: Request body for high-throughput COPY-protocol ingest into a single channel. Timestamps must be globally unique for the channel — no ON CONFLICT handling.

dataPoints.description: Data points to ingest. Each timestamp must be unique within the batch and must not already exist for this channel. The value type must match the channel's declared value type (Double, Integer, String, or Boolean).

storageSizeBytes.description: Total bytes on disk (includes data, indexes, and free space in pre-allocated files).

dataSizeBytes.description: Bytes occupied by all documents in the database (excludes indexes and free space).

collectionCount.description: Number of collections in the database.

ContainerV2.description: Unified, polymorphic wire shape for every container kind under /v2/containers.

revision.description: Write counter (V2a revision). 0 for non-versionable entities.

kind.description: Container family of this container: file | timeseries | structured-data | hdf. Derived from the entity; never numeric.

payload.description: Per-kind read-only field payload. Deterministic key set per kind (see docs/reference/containers.md): file kind → {oid, defaultCollectionIdList}; structured-data kind → {oid}; timeseries kind → {} (no extra fields today).

DataObjectBatchCreateItem.description: One DataObject to create as part of a batch. `collectionAppId` and `name` are required; all other fields are optional.

collectionAppId.description: The appId (UUID v7) of the Collection in which to create this DataObject.

attributes.description: Optional string-to-string attribute map. Keys and values are free-form strings; they are written as Neo4j node properties on the created DataObject.

status.description: Optional initial status. Suggested values: DRAFT, IN_REVIEW, READY, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED. Any free-form string is accepted; the server does not enforce a state machine.

OntologyAlignment.description: A single entry in the Shepard upper-ontology alignment registry. Records the mapping from a Shepard concept to an upper-ontology class IRI, the OWL relationship type, an editorial confidence level, and the aidocs document that established the mapping.

shepardConcept.description: Simple name of the Shepard Java / Neo4j type, e.g. "Collection", "DataObject".

confidence.description: Editorial confidence level for the mapping: HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW.

source.description: aidocs path of the document that established this mapping, e.g. "aidocs/semantics/96-upper-ontology-alignment.md".

createdAt.description: Epoch-millis timestamp when the row was first created by the migration.

neo4jNodeId.description: Neo4j internal node id of the orphan entity — exposed here as a triage handle when appId is null (pre-migration rows).

appId.description: appId (UUID v7) if populated by L2a/L2b.

labels.description: The :BasicEntity sub-label list (e.g. ['Collection', 'BasicEntity']) — useful for triage.

name.description: Entity name if any — passed through from the Named mixin.

mongodb.description: MongoDB (files, avatars, structured data, HDF5 metadata).

ProjectIO.description: Project envelope — a Collection that bundles non-exclusive child Collections.

appId.description: Stable UUID v7 identifier of the underlying Collection.

programmes.description: Programme labels declared on this Project (urn:shepard:programme).

subCollectionCount.description: Number of Collections that carry urn:shepard:partOf = <this appId>.

aggregateDoCount.description: Sum of DataObject counts across all sub-Collections.

lastActivityMillis.description: Maximum {created/updated} timestamp across all sub-Collections; epoch millis.

isProject.description: Always true for endpoints under /v2/projects/{appId}; included for cross-API parity.

effectiveDisplayName.description: Effective rendered display name, with fallbacks applied.

anonymizeInProvenance.description: PROV1l — when true, identity is omitted from :Activity records (GDPR opt-out). Default false.

framesAnalyzed.description: Number of TIFF frames successfully decoded + scored.

framesSkipped.description: Number of TIFF frames skipped (decode error, non-TIFF MIME, zero-pixel frame, …). Skips are logged as WARN.

maxPeakDeltaC.description: Maximum peak-delta-c across all frames, degrees C.

meanOfMeanDeltaC.description: Mean of mean-delta-c across all frames, degrees C.

maxC.description: Absolute maximum temperature observed, degrees C.

thresholdC.description: Threshold-C used for the quality-score denominator.

qualityScore.description: Bundle-level quality score in [0, 1]. 1 = perfectly uniform; 0 = worst frame met or exceeded the threshold.

hotspotCentroidX.description: Bundle-level hot-spot centroid x-coordinate in frame pixel coordinates (weighted average), -1 when no frames.

annotationsWritten.description: Number of SemanticAnnotation rows written by this analysis (bundle-level + DO-level scores).

bundles.description: Optional list of bundle ids to refresh (e.g. ["prov-o","qudt"]). Default: all bundles in the manifest.

force.description: When true, re-imports even when the canonical Turtle hash matches the bundled stub.

activityAppIds.description: One to 100 Activity appId values (UUID v7) whose JSON-LD digests will be anchored.

timestamp.description: Time in nanoseconds since unix epoch.

value.description: A string, an int, a double or a boolean

assetInformation.description: Minimal asset information block per IDTA AAS v3 §7.

description.description: Human-readable description list (langString). Sourced from Collection.description. Absent when the Collection has no description.

submodels.description: Submodel references (IDTA AAS v3 Reference objects). Empty in AAS1a; populated by AAS1b from top-level DataObjects on single-Shell GET. Type changed from List<String> to List<AasReference> in AAS1b Commit 2.

structuredDataReferenceCount.description: Deprecated — use `structuredDataCount` on the /v2/ DataObjectListItemV2 shape instead. This int includes soft-deleted references; the v2 long field counts non-deleted only.

collectionAppId.description: Collection appId the role applies to.

read.description: Caller can read entities under this Collection.

write.description: Caller can create / update entities under this Collection.

manage.description: Caller can edit permissions / delete on this Collection (also implies an owner-like UX role).

isInstanceAdmin.description: Caller carries the `instance-admin` role (dual-sourced from IdP claim AND/OR `:HAS_ROLE` edge, deduped on the JWT principal). The role chip surfaces this as a side-by-side chip so admins can tell they're operating in an elevated context.

items.description: The files on this page, ordered by their position in the group's files[] list.

plugins.description: Plugins whose state is ENABLED on this instance.

predicateUri.description: Absolute IRI of the predicate, e.g. http://semantics.dlr.de/shepard-upper#status.

substrate.description: Authoritative storage substrate: neo4j | timescaledb | postgres | garage.

orcid.description: ORCID identifier, format `NNNN-NNNN-NNNN-NNN[N|X]`. Empty string clears the field; malformed input returns 400. Null = leave unchanged.

displayName.description: User-chosen display name. Empty string clears the override (UI falls back to firstName+lastName, then redacted-username). Null = leave unchanged.

username.description: The user's username (the canonical user identifier).

grantedBy.description: Username (or 'bootstrap') that granted the role. Null for IdP-only grants.

sourceProviderId.description: Id of the storage adapter to migrate files away from.

targetProviderId.description: Id of the storage adapter to migrate files to.

PayloadVersionIO.description: One byte-level version entry for a file stored in a FileContainer.

appId.description: UUID v7 identifier of this version node.

fileOid.description: GridFS ObjectId hex of the stored bytes. Null for presigned-URL uploads.

sha256.description: SHA-256 upper-case hex digest of the uploaded bytes. Null for legacy or presigned-URL uploads.

sizeBytes.description: Byte count of the uploaded payload.

uploadedBy.description: Username of the caller who triggered the upload.

uploadedAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the upload.

UnresolvedEntryIO.description: One unresolved selector in the YAML — operator follow-up checklist row.

line.description: 1-based line number in the YAML payload (best-effort).

side.description: "source" or "target" — which side of the entry did not resolve.

reason.description: Why the selector did not match — typically zero DataObjects.

parentCollectionAppId.description: AppId of the owning Collection — populated for kind=dataobject, null for kind=collection. Enables the frontend to construct the navigation route without a secondary collection-lookup.

templateAppId.description: appId (UUID v7) of the VIEW_RECIPE :ShepardTemplate to render (template-rooted dispatch). Optional: omit it and supply shapeIri + focusFileRefAppId for a file-rooted render. 404 when supplied but not found; 422 when found but templateKind != VIEW_RECIPE.

focusShepardId.description: appId (UUID v7) of the focus DataObject whose timeseries channels are projected through the recipe's channel bindings.

mediaType.description: Desired response media type. Informational — the concrete media is driven by the Accept header.

params.description: Per-call render knobs that vary per request and cannot live in a stored template body, e.g. {"frame":"3","channel":"phase"} or {"mode":"index"}.

confirmPhrase.description: Must be exactly "yes drop everything" to confirm the destructive reset.

RedactableField.description: Closed set of fields on metadata IO documents that can be redacted before bundling. Strings are replaced with [REDACTED]; long[] arrays are emptied.

href.description: URI template of the submit endpoint. {collectionAppId} is filled by the client from context.

violationContract.description: Human-readable note on the 422 violation contract.

ok.description: Whether the ingest run completed without errors.

filesIngested.description: Number of ontology files ingested from the repo.

error.description: Error message when ok=false; null when ok=true.

OntologyBundleIO.description: One pre-seeded or operator-uploaded ontology bundle.

id.description: Bundle slug — unique across built-in + user namespaces.

iriPrefix.description: Canonical IRI prefix the ontology mints terms under.

canonicalUrl.description: Canonical refresh URL (nullable for user-uploaded bundles).

sha256.description: Hex SHA-256 of the bundle bytes.

TypedPredecessorSummary.description: PROV1k read-side — compact predecessor DataObject summary plus the PROV-O / FAIR²R relationship type under which it was linked. relationshipType is always non-null; defaults to 'prov:wasInformedBy' for predecessors created before PROV1k.

predecessorName.description: Display name of the predecessor DataObject.

relationshipType.description: PROV-O or FAIR²R relationship type: 'prov:wasInformedBy' (default / generic), 'prov:wasRevisionOf' (direct revision/correction), 'fair2r:repairs' (rework/NCR-repair).

get.description: APISIMP-GIT-REF-PATH: this path is retired. Use GET /v2/references/{appId}/preview.

get.summary: [v2] List Collections as IDTA AAS v3 Shells (AAS1a).
get.description: Returns one AssetAdministrationShell per Collection the authenticated caller may read. Shells carry `id` (URN from appId), `idShort` (sanitised name), `assetInformation`, optional `description`, and an empty `submodels` list. Submodels are populated by AAS1b. See `aidocs/integrations/52-aas-backend-integration.md §4a`. Uses page-offset pagination (default page=0, pageSize=50, server cap 200).

parameters.description: Zero-based page number (default 0).
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Page size (default 50, server cap 200).
parameters.name: pageSize

200.description: Paged list of Shells readable by the caller. Envelope: { items[], total, page, pageSize }.

patch.summary: [v2] RFC 7396 merge-patch the config for a feature.
patch.description: Resolves the ConfigDescriptor for {feature} and applies an RFC-7396 merge-patch: absent = leave alone, null = clear, value = replace. Returns the updated shape (same as GET). PROV1a's ProvenanceCaptureFilter captures this PATCH as an :Activity row. 404 (problem+json) for an unknown feature; 4xx (problem+json) for a validation failure declared by the descriptor.

400.description: A patched value failed the descriptor's validation (RFC 7807).

patch.description:
Sets / replaces the relationship type for the predecessor edge from the DataObject identified by `dataObjectAppId` to the predecessor identified by `predecessorAppId`. The edge must already exist (use create / merge-patch to add new predecessor links).

Allowed `relationshipType` values:
  - `prov:wasInformedBy` — default / generic informational dependency (the QM1b 'normal' kind)
  - `prov:wasRevisionOf` — direct revision / correction (the QM1b 're-test' kind)
  - `fair2r:repairs` — rework / NCR-repair (the QM1b 'rework' kind)
  - `fair2r:concession` — concession / use-as-is disposition (QM1b)

Idempotent: re-running with the same `relationshipType` is a no-op.

Auth: Write permission on the parent Collection.

Side effects: `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` records an `UPDATE` Activity addressable at `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}`.

patch.summary: [v2] Partially update a Collection (RFC 7396 JSON Merge Patch).
patch.description:
Applies an RFC 7396 JSON Merge Patch to the `:Collection`:
  - Fields PRESENT in the body REPLACE the corresponding fields.
  - Fields ABSENT from the body are LEFT UNCHANGED.
  - Fields set to explicit JSON `null` are CLEARED.

The merged result is Bean-Validated against `CollectionIO` constraints; violations on the final state return 400 (e.g. clearing `name` would produce a constraint violation because `name` is `@NotBlank`).

Example: rename a Collection without touching other fields — 
`{"name": "renamed"}`. Clear the description — `{"description": null}`.

Auth: Write permission on the Collection.

Content-Type: prefer `application/merge-patch+json` (the RFC 7396 type); the endpoint also accepts `application/json` for clients that can't set the dedicated type.

Side effects: ProvenanceCaptureFilter records an `UPDATE` Activity. The `:Version` HEAD is advanced; the previous state is reachable via `GET /shepard/api/collections/{id}/versions`.

requestBody.description: Partial Collection (RFC 7396). Every field is optional; absent fields are preserved.

400.description: Bad request — body is not a JSON object or validation failed.

Auth: Read on the Collection. `404` is returned for unknown appIds *before* the auth check — distinguishing 'doesn't exist' from 'exists but you can't see it' is intentionally not done (timing-safe).

Next step: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` for the DataObject list, or `PATCH /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}` for an RFC 7396 merge-patch update.
get.x-agent-hint: Top-level container. appId is the stable identifier for all v2 calls.

Marks the `:Collection` deleted (`deleted=true`) plus every reachable DataObject, Reference, LabJournalEntry and Snapshot. The underlying containers (FileContainer / TimeseriesContainer / StructuredDataContainer) stay — they are top-level entities, not nested under a Collection, so deleting a Collection orphans its references but does not destroy the payload bytes. Use the container-kind DELETE endpoints for that.

Idempotency: deleting a non-existent or already-deleted Collection returns 204 without error.

Auth: Write permission on the Collection.

Side effects: ProvenanceCaptureFilter records a `DELETE` Activity. Subscriptions attached to the Collection fire on this write.

get.summary: [v2] Generate a FAIR DMP snippet for a Collection.
get.description:
Returns a copy-paste-ready Markdown Data Management Plan (DMP) block pre-filled with all FAIR-relevant fields from the Collection (`name`, `description`, `license`, `accessRights`) and its DataObjects (`createdByOrcid`, `embargoEndDate`, reference kinds). Designed for researchers who need to fill in DFG / EU Horizon Europe DMP forms.

**Content negotiation:**
- `Accept: text/markdown` (default): returns the Markdown block as plain text.
- `Accept: application/json`: returns a JSON wrapper with `collectionAppId`, `collectionName`, `snippet` (the same Markdown), and `missingFields` (list of FAIR fields that are null/blank and should be set).

**Missing-fields detection:** the `missingFields` array (JSON variant) lists field names that improve the DMP statement if populated: `license`, `accessRights`, `orcid` (no DataObject has a `createdByOrcid`), `description` (Collection is blank).

**Auth:** Read permission on the Collection. `404` for unknown appIds; `403` for callers without Read access; `401` when unauthenticated.

**PID note:** persistent identifier (PID) lookup is not implemented in this version. The snippet always emits 'no PID assigned'; see `aidocs/16` FAIR7 row.

**No side effects:** pure read projection — no entity writes, no migrations required.

200.description: DMP snippet as Markdown (text/markdown) or JSON wrapper (application/json).

Query strategy: when the `resource_labels` fulltext index exists (created by migration `V44__Add_fulltext_index_Resource_labels.cypher`), the endpoint uses `db.index.fulltext.queryNodes` for fast prefix matching. patch.summary: [v2] Partial-update the caller's UI preferences.
patch.description:
Applies an RFC 7396 JSON Merge Patch to the caller's preference map. Keys with non-null string values are set or updated. Keys with explicit JSON `null` values are REMOVED. Keys absent from the body are PRESERVED. The endpoint returns the resulting map after the patch is applied.

Example: set theme and remove a stale key — `{"theme": "dark", "defaultPageSize": null}`.

Known UI keys (open-world — unknown string keys are also accepted): `theme` (light / dark / auto), `language` (BCP-47), `timeZone` (IANA), `dateFormat`, `defaultPageSize` (integer-as-string), `defaultLandingPage` (URL path), `ui.advancedMode` ("true"/"false"), `ui.showOrcidBadge` ("true"/"false"). Plugins may stake out their own keys (recommended namespacing: `<plugin>.<setting>`).

Content-Type: prefer `application/merge-patch+json`; the endpoint also accepts `application/json` for clients that can't set the dedicated type.

Auth: any authenticated user — preferences are scoped to the caller's `:User` node and never affect other users.

Constraint: non-string non-null values return 400.

Returns 409 if the plan was already used, if a concurrent import is running, or if the collection changed since the plan was validated. Returns 410 if the plan expired (TTL 24 h) or is a pre-IMP2 plan (no stored manifest). Returns 422 if the plan was INVALIDATED (had hard validation errors).

Example full body: `{"name": "run-001", "description": "hot-fire sequence", "startedAt": "2026-05-01T10:00:00Z", "endedAt": "2026-05-01T10:30:00Z", "index": 0}`.

get.summary: [v2] Process-chain timeline (swimlane chronograph) for a Collection.
get.description:
Returns a swimlane envelope where each lane is a distinct process-type (derived from the `urn:shepard:mffd:process-type` SemanticAnnotation on each DataObject) and each bin within a lane counts the DataObjects whose `createdAt` falls in that bin's day window. NCR (`NCR_OPEN`, `CONCESSION_PENDING`) and `REJECTED` counts are overlaid per-bin so the UI can render stacked colour-coded bars.

DataObjects without a `urn:shepard:mffd:process-type` annotation collect into a synthetic `unclassified` lane — so the Timeline still surfaces activity for non-MFFD Collections (LUMEN, home-showcase) without misleadingly hiding it.

Adaptive bin size: `?binSizeDays=` accepts 1, 7, 30, 90, or 365 (non-ladder values snap upward). When the campaign span divided by the requested bin size would produce more than ~730 bins per lane, the server auto-coarsens upward through the ladder until it fits. The actual bin size used is echoed in the response's `binSizeDays` field.

Auth: Read permission on the Collection (inherited by DataObjects).

Performance target: ≤ 2 s for an MFFD-scale Collection (≈ 8.2k DataObjects, 5 lanes). Cached for 5 minutes via Cache-Control.
get.x-agent-hint: Process-step swimlane view of a Collection. Use to surface campaign cadence + NCR clusters at scale; pair with the DataObjects list endpoint for drill-down.

parameters.description: Histogram bin width in days. Accepted ladder values: 1, 7, 30, 90, 365. Non-ladder values snap upward to the next ladder step. When the campaign span would produce more than ~730 bins per lane, the server auto-coarsens upward through the ladder until it fits. Default 1. The actual bin size used is echoed in the response's binSizeDays field.
parameters.name: binSizeDays

200.description: Timeline envelope with lanes + bins. `lanes` is empty when the Collection has no non-deleted DataObjects. `binSizeDays` reflects the actually-used bin size (may differ from the request when auto-coarsened).

post.summary: [v2] Check whether two DataObject sets are mutually independent.
post.description:
Runs two checks against the two supplied DataObject appId sets:

1. **Provenance ancestry** — are there any DataObjects that are    common ancestors (within 10 hops) of members in both sets?
2. **Shared annotations** — do any members of setA and setB share an    annotation key-value pair (strict equality on both key and value)?

`independent: true` is returned only when both checks find no overlap.

**Typical use case:** validate that a training set and a test set used for AI/ML training do not share data lineage.

**Hop cap:** the ancestor walk is bounded at 10 hops. Provenance chains longer than 10 levels are not covered by this check — the result is best-effort, not a formal mathematical proof.

**Auth:** any authenticated user. The endpoint returns only structural metadata (ancestor appIds, annotation keys/values) — no DataObject content.

200.description: Check completed. Inspect `independent` and the two lists for details.

400.description: Request body missing or setA/setB is null/empty.

The response body is NOT recorded in :Activity — PROV1a's ProvenanceCaptureFilter captures only the request method + path + status, never response bodies, so the plaintext never enters the audit trail. The plaintext is also never logged server-side; only the masked fingerprint (first 8 hex chars of the SHA-256) appears in INFO logs.

The merged result is then Bean-Validated; constraint violations on the final state return 400. Returns the full updated entity. Accepts both application/merge-patch+json (preferred, per RFC 7396) and application/json in /v1/; future /v2/ APIs will require application/merge-patch+json.

post.summary: [v2] Roll back a single file's storage-adapter swap.
post.description:
FS1e3 — per-file rollback. Re-writes the file's bytes from the current adapter (providerId) back to the previous adapter (previousProviderId), using the preserved previousLocator, then restores providerId and clears the FS1e3 bookkeeping fields (previousProviderId, previousLocator, migratedAt, migrationHmac). Synchronous — the response carries the outcome.

Refuses with 409 when the :ShepardFile row's previousProviderId is null (never migrated, or already rolled back). Refuses with 404 when the appId is unknown. Caller must hold the instance-admin role.

Note: this endpoint re-writes bytes; it does NOT delete from the current adapter. The orphaned current-adapter bytes are catalogued by a future 'sweep-orphans' verb (runbook §17.E).

parameters.description: The :ShepardFile.appId (UUID v7) to roll back.

200.description: Rollback succeeded — providerId restored, bookkeeping cleared.

409.description: The :ShepardFile has nothing to roll back (previousProviderId is null).

[v2] Execute a read-only SPARQL SELECT or ASK query (POST form variant).
post.description:
Identical semantics to the GET variant, but accepts the query as a URL-encoded form body (`Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, field name `query`), following SPARQL 1.1 Protocol §2.1.3.

Use this variant when the SPARQL query is too long to fit in a URL — many HTTP proxies and load balancers reject query strings longer than 2 KB. POST form bodies carry no such limit.

All constraints and error responses are identical to the GET form: only `SELECT` and `ASK` are permitted; auth, repository-type dispatch, and upstream error mapping behave the same way.

200.description: W3C SPARQL Results JSON (`application/sparql-results+json`) — raw SELECT or ASK result from the backend.

400.description: Query is empty, missing from the form body, or is a mutation form (CONSTRUCT / INSERT / DELETE / UPDATE …).

401.description: Authentication required (no JWT and no X-API-KEY).

404.description: No SemanticRepository with that appId, or the repository is deleted.

501.description: Repository type does not support SPARQL (JSKOS or SKOSMOS).

502.description: Backend SPARQL endpoint returned a non-200 HTTP status.

503.description: Backend SPARQL endpoint could not be reached (connection refused, timeout, or invalid URL).

get.summary: [v2] Execute a read-only SPARQL SELECT or ASK query (GET form).
get.description:
Accepts a SPARQL query via the `query` URL parameter and proxies it to the backend identified by `repoAppId`, following SPARQL 1.1 Protocol §2.1.2.

Only `SELECT` and `ASK` query forms are permitted. Any mutation form (`CONSTRUCT`, `INSERT`, `DELETE`, `UPDATE`, …) is rejected with 400 before reaching the backend.

Repository-type dispatch:
  - `INTERNAL` — proxies to the n10s (neosemantics) HTTP SPARQL endpoint on     the Neo4j HTTP port (default `http://localhost:7474`). Configurable via     `shepard.semantic.internal.http-url`.
  - `SPARQL` — proxies to the external endpoint URL stored on the entity.     The caller's `Authorization` header is stripped before forwarding to     prevent credential leakage.
  - `JSKOS` / `SKOSMOS` — returns 501; these repository types do not     implement the SPARQL protocol.

Auth: any authenticated shepard user may query any SemanticRepository. There is no per-entity permission check beyond authentication, matching the posture of the v1 SemanticRepository catalogue.

Response: W3C SPARQL Results JSON (`application/sparql-results+json`) passed through verbatim from the backend. Non-200 backend responses are mapped to 502 (backend error) or 503 (unreachable).

parameters.description: URL-encoded SPARQL 1.1 SELECT or ASK query string. Must be non-empty — null or blank returns 400. Only `SELECT` and `ASK` query forms are accepted; any mutation form (`CONSTRUCT`, `INSERT`, `DELETE`, `UPDATE`, …) is rejected with 400 before reaching the backend. For queries longer than ~2 KB prefer the POST form variant (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`, field name `query`) to avoid proxy URL-length limits.
parameters.name: query

200.description: W3C SPARQL Results JSON (`application/sparql-results+json`) — raw SELECT or ASK result from the backend.

400.description: Query is empty, shorter than 1 character, or is a mutation form (CONSTRUCT / INSERT / DELETE / UPDATE …).

401.description: Authentication required (no JWT and no X-API-KEY).

404.description: No SemanticRepository with that appId, or the repository is deleted.

501.description: Repository type does not support SPARQL (JSKOS or SKOSMOS).

503.description: Backend SPARQL endpoint could not be reached (connection refused, timeout, or invalid URL).

Returns one page of non-deleted `:Snapshot` entities for the Collection identified by `collectionAppId`, ordered by `snapshotCapturedAtMs` DESC (newest first).

Each `SnapshotIO` carries: `appId`, `name`, `description`, `snapshotCapturedAtMs` (millis-epoch), `snapshotCreatedByUsername`, and `entryCount` (number of VersionableEntities recorded in the manifest).

Pagination: omit `page` / `size` to get the first 50; supply both to paginate. `size` capped at 200 server-side.

Auth: Read on the Collection.

Next step: `GET /v2/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/manifest` for the per-entity entries of a specific Snapshot, or `GET /v2/snapshots/{a}/diff/{b}` to compare two Snapshots.

post.summary: [v2] Create a Snapshot of a Collection's current state (V2b).
post.description:
Creates a point-in-time `:Snapshot` of the Collection identified by `collectionAppId`. The server walks the Collection subtree up to 15 hops deep and records the current `revision` counter of every `:VersionableEntity` (DataObject, Reference, …) it reaches. The resulting Snapshot is immutable — future writes to the underlying entities don't affect what the Snapshot reports.

Body fields:
  - `name` (required, non-blank) — operator-chosen label, e.g. `"v1.0"`, `"pre-publication"`, `"after-fix"`.
  - `description` (optional) — free-form, CommonMark/GFM.

Example body: `{"name": "v1.0", "description": "Initial publication-ready state."}`.

Auth: Write on the Collection (snapshotting is a state-recording mutation on the snapshot subgraph, even though it doesn't modify the snapshotted entities themselves).

Side effects: a `:Snapshot` node plus one `:SnapshotEntry` per reachable VersionableEntity. ProvenanceCaptureFilter records a `CREATE` Activity addressable at `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}`.

Next step: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/snapshots` to list, `GET /v2/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}` for metadata, or `GET /v2/snapshots/{snapshotAppId}/manifest` for the per-entity entries.

400.description: name is required and must be non-blank.

403.description: Caller lacks Write permission on the Collection.

get.summary: [v2] List Collections the caller may read.
get.description:
Returns a page of `:Collection` entities (`CollectionIO` JSON shape) that the authenticated caller has Read permission on. The page is unordered by default; supply `orderBy` (one of the `DataObjectAttributes` enum: `createdAt`, `name`, `status`, …) with `orderDesc=true` for a sorted result.

Pagination: omit `page` / `pageSize` to get the first 50; supply both to paginate. The server caps `pageSize` at 200 to avoid unbounded result sets.

Filtering: `name` does a case-insensitive substring match.

Each returned `CollectionV2IO` carries `appId` (canonical UUID v7) as the stable identifier; use the matching `/v2/...` endpoints for follow-up calls.

Auth: no role gate — the result is filtered server-side to entities the caller may Read, so an unauthorised caller simply gets a smaller list (not 403).

Next step: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}` to fetch a specific Collection with its DataObjects expanded.

parameters.description: Optional case-insensitive substring filter on Collection name. Omit to return all collections the caller can read.
parameters.name: name

parameters.description: 0-based page index. Default 0. Negative values are rejected (400).
parameters.name: page

parameters.description: Page size. Default 50; clamped server-side to [1, 200]. Negative values are rejected (400).
parameters.name: pageSize

200.description: Paged envelope: items + total + page + pageSize. Header X-Total-Count = total count before paging (kept during deprecation window, APISIMP-PAGINATION-ENVELOPE).

400.description: Bean Validation rejected the query — `page` or `pageSize` is negative.

401.description: Authentication required (no JWT and no X-API-KEY).

post.summary: [v2] Create a new Collection.
post.description:
Creates a `:Collection` and returns the full entity in the 201 body. The server mints both `appId` (UUID v7, canonical) and `id` (legacy long); the caller becomes the owner with Manage permission.

Body fields:
  - `name` (string, required, non-blank).
  - `description` (string, optional, supports CommonMark + GFM).
  - `attributes` (string-to-string map, optional; keys must not contain the delimiter characters `Space, Comma, Point, Slash`).
  - `status` (optional, one of `DRAFT`, `IN_REVIEW`, `READY`, `PUBLISHED`, `ARCHIVED`).
  - `defaultFileContainerId` (optional, legacy long id of a FileContainer to serve as the Collection's default).

Example minimal body: `{"name": "My experiment"}`.
Example with attributes: `{"name": "TR-001", "description": "Hot-fire run", "attributes": {"campaign": "Q3", "site": "Lampoldshausen"}, "status": "DRAFT"}`.

Auth: any authenticated user can create a Collection (the request is authenticated via JWT or `X-API-KEY`). The created Collection's permission-type defaults to `PRIVATE`; flip it to `PUBLIC` via `PUT /shepard/api/collections/{id}/permissions` to make it visible to all other users.

Side effects: `ProvenanceCaptureFilter` records a `CREATE` Activity addressable at `GET /v2/provenance/entity/{appId}`. A `:Version` node is created with `Constants.HEAD` as its initial version.

Next step: `POST /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects` to add DataObjects, or `PATCH /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}` to set additional metadata.

Collection-scoped alias for `GET /v2/data-objects/{appId}/rdf`. The `collectionAppId` path segment is accepted for URL consistency with the collection-scoped CRUD surface but is not independently validated against the DataObject's actual parent — the delegate resolves the parent Collection server-side and enforces Read permission there. post.description: Parses the template body's `dataobjects[0].attributes` and applies them to the new DataObject. Records a `:CREATED_FROM_TEMPLATE` relationship back to the template. Requires Write permission on the Collection. The template must be non-retired; if the Collection has a non-empty allow-list the template must appear in it. When the flattened template body includes a `shapeGraph` (Turtle) field, the candidate DataObject's attributes are validated against it before creation — 422 is returned on violation (V2CONV-B2 / aidocs/platform/191 §3).

422.description: The candidate DataObject violates the template's SHACL shape graph. The problem-JSON carries a structured violations[] extension ({path, value?, constraint?, message} per finding; path = the SHACL resultPath predicate IRI, matching the form descriptor's fields[].path).

get.description:
Returns a page of `:DataObject` entities belonging to the Collection identified by `collectionAppId`. Each row in the response carries the full `DataObjectIO` fields plus three per-kind reference counts: `timeseriesCount`, `fileCount`, `structuredDataCount`. Counts reflect non-deleted references only and are computed in a single Cypher round-trip (no N+1 queries).

Pagination: omit `page` / `size` to get the first 50; supply both to paginate. `size` capped at 200 server-side.

Filtering: `name` does a case-insensitive substring match. Each row also carries `referenceIds[]` (legacy long ids of all refs) and `childrenIds[]` (direct child DOs).

Optional enrichment via `?include=time-bounds`: adds `timeBoundsStart` and `timeBoundsEnd` (epoch nanoseconds) to each item, reflecting the earliest and latest data-point timestamps across all timeseries channels. Null on items with no timeseries data. Omitted from the response entirely when `?include=time-bounds` is not requested.

**Payload diet (DB-OPT5).** By default the list response drops fields the collection-detail UI never reads — `description`, `attributes`, and the three deprecated `int` count siblings (`timeseriesReferenceCount`, `fileBundleCount`, `structuredDataReferenceCount`). Use `?include=full` to opt back into the full wire shape (transitional safety valve until a breaking-version bump can drop the deprecated ints unconditionally). For finer-grained control, pass `?fields=appId,name,createdAt,...` (flat CSV of field names; GitHub REST convention). `id`, `appId`, and `name` are always included as resource identity. Unknown field names return 400 with the offending name in the body. Dotted-path nested selection (e.g. `attributes.bench`) is not supported in this iteration — see DB-OPT5-NESTED in the backlog.

Auth: Read on the parent Collection. DataObjects inherit Collection permissions; there is no per-DO permission gate.

Next step: `GET /v2/collections/{collectionAppId}/data-objects/{dataObjectAppId}` to fetch a specific DataObject with full reference detail.
get.x-agent-hint: Returns paginated list. Use ?include=time-bounds for timeseries coverage bars. timeseriesCount/fileCount/structuredDataCount give reference counts per kind. Default payload drops description/attributes; use ?include=full or ?fields= for finer control.

parameters.description: Comma-separated response modifiers. `time-bounds` — populates `timeBoundsStart`/`timeBoundsEnd` per DataObject (costs one extra TimescaleDB round-trip). `full` — opts back into the full (pre-DB-OPT5) wire shape including deprecated fields.
parameters.name: include

Drives the lazy collection-sidebar tree: fetch one hierarchy level per expand. Composes with `page`/`pageSize`/`fields`/`name`/`status`/`annotationFilter`. Mutually exclusive with `topLevel=true` (when both are given, `topLevel` wins). An unknown `parentAppId` yields an empty page.
